channel.title: raspberry pi on I'm Keith Kee KW
channel.description: Recent content in raspberry pi on I'm Keith Kee KW

item.title: How to Set Up Samba Server in Raspberry Pi
item.description: Ensure the Raspberry Pi’s repositories are up-to-date by performing the command below in the terminal: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade -y Next, proceed to install all the required packages from the remote repositories for Samba by running the command below: sudo apt-get install samba samba-common-bin After the installation has been completed, we can proceed to create a folder to be shared via the Samba Server.

item.title: Installation of PostgreSQL in Raspberry Pi
item.description: Installation Run the following command to install the packages: sudo apt install postgresql libpq-dev postgresql-client postgresql-client-common -y When that’s complete, switch to the Postgres user to configure the database: sudo su postgres Create a database user: createuser postgres -P --interactive Configuration for Remote Access Configuration on postgresql.config need to be altered in order for pgAdmin to access the cluster from a different computer within the network.
